name: gitlab-automation
description: Automate GitLab project management, issues, merge requests, pipelines, branches, and user operations via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Create, update, and search GitLab issues with labels, assignees, and milestones
List and filter merge requests by state, author, reviewer, and labels
Monitor CI/CD pipeline status and retrieve job details
Manage project branches and view commit history
Search GitLab users and list project members
Create new GitLab projects with visibility and namespace settings
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is Markdown documentation for GitLab automation via Rube MCP (Composio). All 301 static analyzer 'external_commands' findings are FALSE POSITIVE - the detected backticks are Markdown code formatting (e.g., `RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS`), not Ruby shell execution. The single 'network' finding is documentation instructing users to configure an MCP endpoint, not actual network code. No executable code present - this is a documentation-only skill with no security risks.
valueStatement: Manage GitLab projects, issues, merge requests, and pipelines without manual API calls. This skill provides structured workflows for common GitLab operations through Composio's GitLab toolkit.
userTitle: Automate GitLab workflows and CI/CD pipelines
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server to be configured and connected
Some features require GitLab Premium or Ultimate plans
Admin-only user management filters are not available to regular users
Rate limits apply based on GitLab plan (300-2000 requests/minute)

useCases.title: Development Team Lead
useCases.description: Track sprint progress by listing issues by label, assigning team members, and monitoring merge request approvals without leaving the AI chat interface.

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer
useCases.description: Monitor CI/CD pipeline health across projects, identify failed jobs, and trigger pipeline reruns automatically when deployments fail.

useCases.title: Project Manager
useCases.description: Generate status reports by querying project activity, listing recent merge requests, and tracking issue resolution rates across multiple projects.

promptTemplates.title: List open critical bugs
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for all open issues in the project 'my-group/my-app' labeled as 'bug' and 'critical'. Show me the title, assignee, and creation date for each.

promptTemplates.title: Review pending merge requests
promptTemplates.prompt: List all merge requests in 'my-group/my-app' that are assigned to me and have not been merged yet. Include the source branch, target branch, and number of comments on each.

promptTemplates.title: Check failed pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Find the most recent failed pipeline in 'my-group/my-app' on the 'main' branch. Show me which jobs failed and their error messages.

promptTemplates.title: Create release branch
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new branch called 'release/v1.2.0' in 'my-group/my-app' from the 'main' branch. Then list all branches to confirm it was created.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Use filters (state, labels, date ranges) to narrow result sets and respect rate limits
Verify project IDs with GITLAB_GET_PROJECT before performing write operations
antiPatterns: Do not use the labels field to add labels incrementally - it replaces all existing labels. Use add_labels instead.
Avoid unfiltered listing of all projects or pipelines - always use search or status filters
Do not assume numeric IDs - use URL-encoded paths or verify IDs with a lookup call first

faq.question: What do I need to set up before using this skill?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client configuration. Then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'gitlab' and complete OAuth authorization if prompted.

faq.question: What is the difference between project ID and project path?
faq.answer: Project ID is a numeric identifier (e.g., 123). Project path is the URL-encoded namespace/name format (e.g., 'my-group%2Fmy-project'). Most tools accept both formats.

faq.question: Why are some features not working for my GitLab account?
faq.answer: Some features require GitLab Premium or Ultimate plans. Admin-only features like user management filters require administrator permissions. Check your GitLab plan and permissions.

faq.question: How do I assign an issue to multiple users?
faq.answer: Use the assignee_ids parameter with an array of user IDs. First call GITLAB_LIST_PROJECT_USERS or GITLAB_GET_USERS to find the numeric user IDs, then pass them as [123, 456].

faq.question: What happens if I exceed GitLab rate limits?
faq.answer: GitLab enforces per-user rate limits (300-2000 requests/minute). If exceeded, API calls will return 429 errors. Use filters to reduce result sizes and add delays between bulk operations.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with self-hosted GitLab instances?
faq.answer: This skill works with GitLab instances configured in Rube MCP. Self-hosted instances require proper connection setup through RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with the correct GitLab URL.
